_note: ORIGINAL content. Think C++ and the Google C++ Class outline informed coverage only; no prose, examples, or exercises were copied.
title: Operators
summary: Calculate values and combine boolean conditions.
refresher: C++ arithmetic follows precedence rules, comparisons produce booleans, and `&&`, `||`, and `!` combine logic. Integer division discards the fractional part.

blocks.body: C++ arithmetic follows precedence rules, comparisons produce booleans, and `&&`, `||`, and `!` combine logic. Integer division discards the fractional part. Calculate values and combine boolean conditions.

An operator uses the types of both operands. Two integers divided together produce an integer result, even if that result is later stored in a double. Convert an operand before division when the fractional part matters. Logical operators also short-circuit, so the right side may not run.

blocks.caption: Two integer operands produce integer division, while the double operand keeps the fraction.
Convert before the operation that would lose information.

blocks.body: With total seven and count two, the wrong expression performs integer division first and produces three. Assignment changes three into `3.0` but cannot restore the lost half. The corrected expression makes the division itself use floating-point arithmetic.

blocks.title: C++ detail to check
blocks.body: Use `==` for comparison. A single equals sign assigns a value, and an assignment inside a condition can silently alter program state.

blocks.why: Multiplication happens first, leaving ten minus six.
The remainder after two groups of four is one.
